_comment: Recall-librarian fail-closed permission posture (piece C). Deploys to ~/librarian/.claude/settings.json. THE CONFINEMENT (keepalive review r2, HIGH-1/HIGH-2 — ALLOWLIST model): the PreToolUse recall-gate is registered with matcher "*", so it fires for EVERY tool call and is the SOLE decider — it ALLOWS only an exact `mxr ask --scope research|fitness "<safe question>"` (Bash) and DENIES everything else, including every non-Bash tool, structurally. This does NOT depend on the deny-list below staying exhaustive as Claude Code adds tools (the enumeration model previously missed a live write-capable tool, DesignSync). The deny-list is retained as BELT (deny-first precedence: a settings `deny` also overrides any hook `allow`) — Bash stays un-denied so a valid recall can be allowed. MCP: `disableClaudeAiConnectors:true` blocks claude.ai connectors; the launch ALSO sets CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MCP=1 (README) to load zero MCP servers — `mcp__*` here is belt. Edit the hook path if the checkout is not /Users/stevenfernandez/code/active/myndaix-runtime.
